text.title: Runtime Hallucination Detection Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Runtime Hallucination Detection application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Decompose output into atomic claims; check each independently. 'Sentence-level' is the wrong granularity; claims are the unit of fact.
rule.rationale: SelfCheckGPT + factuality research: claim-level decomposition catches errors sentence-level checks miss.

rule.statement: Each claim must be attributable to a retrieval chunk. No attribution = no claim. Show the attribution to the user when stakes are high.
rule.rationale: Retrieval-attribution check is the cheapest hallucination filter and the most explainable.

rule.statement: For critical fields (price, date, identifier, dosage), generate N=3 samples and require consistency. Inconsistency = abstain.
rule.rationale: Self-consistency literature: critical fields benefit from N=3-5 sampling; consistency rate is the calibration signal.

rule.statement: If attribution score < threshold OR self-consistency fails, ABSTAIN. Show 'I don't have a confident answer' + escalate to human / additional retrieval.
rule.rationale: Abstaining is the only safe action in low-evidence regimes; fabricating an answer is the antipattern.

rule.statement: Track abstain rate + post-deployment ground-truth corrections. Spike in either = retrieval quality drift OR model drift; alarm + investigate.
rule.rationale: Hallucination patterns shift with model version + corpus drift; explicit monitoring catches before damage.
